I called preflop intending to set‑mine and this sizing is cheap enough to continue for that plan: calling 700 leaves me ~1,200 behind (~12BB) and keeps the pot manageable. Claude’s range from UTG will include a lot of Kx and some strong Broadway hands, so I’m rarely currently ahead, but I still have ~8–9% to hit a set by the river and some chance to win at showdown vs weaker/air hands if opponents check behind. Raising here with an underpair is speculative and committing now would be poor; folding gives up too much equity for the price. Plan: call to see the turn; if I hit a 5 I’ll commit, otherwise I’ll fold to heavy turn aggression (large bets/raises) and proceed cautiously vs continued small bets or a checked turn.

I entered the pot with pocket Fours strictly to set-mine. The flop comes [7h Ks 9c], missing my set and presenting a board with cards higher than my pair. Claude (UTG) has fired a substantial continuation bet of 700 into a pot of 1050 (approx 66% pot) into three opponents, signaling a strong hand (likely top pair or better). Minni has called this bet. With only an underpair and 2 outs to improve, my equity is negligible (~4%), far below the pot odds required to call. Sticking to the 'set or fold' plan, this is a clear fold.

Middle pair top kicker (99 with A) on a K-9-7 rainbow board is a strong hand in a multiway pot from BB defense vs loose-aggressive UTG raiser Claude. Pot is 2450, facing 700 bet (29% pot) after Minni's call (short stack ~1200 left, likely capped weak pair/draw). Pot odds are excellent (~3.5:1 direct, plus implied from short Minni and deep Claude). Equity strong vs Claude's polarized UTG range (missed K/high card bluffs/draws fold, hits like Kx/Ax/KQ we beat except sets/top two; vs Minni even better). Raising risks folding out worse (Minni folds, Claude calls with better/folds bluffs we want to keep calling), commits OOP multiway. Calling controls pot, realizes equity, induces Claude bluffs/overbets on turn (his LAG style), traps better hands. Fold turn if raised big or bad card. Consistent with pre-flop speculative call and flop check plan.

On the turn, the board pairs the king with Kc, creating a paired board (Ks Kc 9c 7h). I hold middle pair (9s) with the nut ace kicker (As 9d), which is still very strong multiway against Claude's likely polarized continuation range (strong Kx/AK, bluffs/draws) and Minni's capped calling range (weak pairs, draws now with ~1200 behind). Equity remains high (~40-50% vs Claude, higher vs Minni), especially with implied odds from Minni's short stack and dead money in pot of 3150. OOP first to act in 3-way pot, checking accomplishes multiple goals: pot control to avoid bloating further OOP, induces Claude's frequent bluffs/overbets (LAG history), allows trapping stronger hands (sets, two-pair KKxx unlikely), balances with check-folds/bluffs, and sets up check-call or check-raise dynamics favoring equity realization. Donking risks check-raises from Claude's strong hands while folding out worse calling ranges (Minni especially); raising less effective OOP multiway. Consistent with flop check-call plan for speculative BB defense turning into a strong but vulnerable made hand. Plan to check-call most bets or check-raise large overbets.

Turn brought Kc giving me trip kings with queen kicker - a very strong hand. Board is now 7h Ks 9c Kc with a club flush draw possible. Both opponents checked to me. I should bet for value to extract from worse kings, pairs (77, 99), straight draws, and flush draws. Betting around 55% pot for value while keeping calling range wide.

I’m holding 5♠5♦ on a 7♥ K♠ 9♣ K♣ board with 1,200 behind and Claude’s turn bet forces me to call 1,200 (all‑in) to win a 4,850 pot. Claude opened UTG, bet the flop and now barrels a big turn — line strongly consistent with a King or other very strong value hand. Pot odds require ~1,200/(4,850+1,200) ≈ 19.8% equity to break even. My realistic equity is tiny: only two fives (≈4.3%) to make a full house versus a Kx, and little showdown value vs trips/quads. I planned to fold to heavy turn aggression if I missed the set, and this is exactly that spot. Calling would be -EV and risk my short stack when I should wait for a better double‑up opportunity, so I fold.

Heads-up OOP on turn with As9d (middle pair, nut ace kicker) facing Claude's 1700 bet into 4850 pot (~35% pot) after pairing the kings (board 7h Ks 9c Kc). Pot odds excellent (~2.85:1 direct, plus implied vs deep LAG Claude). Hand retains strong equity (~40-45% vs his polarized UTG continuation range: beats bluffs/missed draws like QJ/JT/AQ/AKs/JTs, many Kx worse than AK like KQ/KJ/KT, AK often; loses to sets KK/99/77, K9/two-pair but those thin). Claude's LAG history (frequent aggression pre/flop as seen in hands like #6, #29, #37) suggests bluff-heavy turn bet after multiway flop call, exploiting board pair. Folding too tight (top of calling range); raising OOP commits ~45% stack (to min 3400 leaves ~4100) risks cooler vs better Kx/sets and folds worse. Calling consistent with flop/turn check-call plan for BB defense, controls pot OOP, realizes equity by charging bluffs, traps strong hands, sets up river check-call/raise (river blockers/dead money favor). Effective stacks allow river flexibility (~5800 behind post-call into ~8350 pot). Plan: Call, check river unless bomb/brick, fold to huge overbet or call small/value bet.

I have trip Kings with Queen kicker. The river Ace is interesting - it could have helped Elon if he has Ax (now two pair), which he might call a value bet with. Hands that beat me are AK, 99, 77 (full houses), but these are relatively unlikely given the action. Elon has called two streets so has something - likely a King, pocket pair, or was drawing. I should value bet to extract from worse trips, Aces up, and stubborn 9x or 7x. Betting around 50% pot to get calls from his medium-strength hands.

River Ad completes top two pair (AA KK with 9 kicker) heads-up OOP vs Claude's 3-barrel (UTG LAG, deep 16k stack, history of multi-street aggression e.g., #6,#29,#37,#52). Pot 9750, facing 3200 (~33% pot), pot odds require ~25% equity (call leaves 2625 into 12950, ~0.2 SPR). Range polarized: Value (60-70%) crushes (Kx trips/full: AK/KQ/KJ/KT all KKKxx > us; sets KK/99/77/K9 fullhouses/quads; two-pair unlikely); Ax bluffs improve to AA KK better kicker (AQ/AJ/AT); pure air (QJ/JT/OTD ~20-30%) narrow/weak for 3-bet sizing. Equity ~20-30% (beats tiny bluff slice, loses to value core), < pot odds. Calling OOP commits low-SPR stack, risks brutal reverse implied odds vs better Ax/Kx. Folding preserves 5825 mid-tourney (90 hands left, 6 players), exploits future spots vs LAG Claude (steal blinds/3-bet light). Consistent BB defense plan: speculative pre/flop/turn check-calls folded correctly here vs scare paired board + Ad villain bomb. Discipline > heroics.

I have 5♥4♦ in UTG with a 1,200 stack (~12 BB). This is an early-position, marginal hand that is easily dominated by typical calling/shoving ranges from the players behind (Claude, FiveTwo, Pro all have deep stacks). With only 12 BB I should be in a push-or-fold mindset — but 5-4o UTG is outside a profitable shove range, and a limp or tiny raise loses most of its fold equity while exposing me to 3-bets and dominated all-ins. Preserving my stack and waiting for a better spot (stronger holding or later position) is the +EV play here.

We’re first in from middle position after UTG folds, with A♣Q♣ (strong suited Broadway with good playability and blocker effects versus 3-bets). With several medium/short stacks behind and blinds that will defend, I want to take initiative and build a pot for value while still keeping a manageable risk size. A standard open here is best; folding is too tight and limping wastes the hand’s strength.

I am in the Cutoff with King-Queen offsuit (KQo). The player in Middle Position (FiveTwo) has opened for 300 (3bb). KQo is a strong broadway hand that plays well in position, but facing an MP open, a 3-bet risks isolating me against a stronger range (AK, AQ, JJ+) or facing a 4-bet which would force a fold. Calling allows me to keep the opponent's range wider, including hands I dominate (KJ, QJ) and utilize my position post-flop. Given the stack depths (I have ~73bb), flat calling is a standard and profitable line to realize equity without bloating the pot prematurely.

I am on the button holding 7d 4s, which is a very weak hand. FiveTwo has opened for 3x the big blind and Pro has called. Even with the positional advantage, this hand lacks the equity and post-flop playability to justify a call or a bluff 3-bet. Saving my chips for a better spot is the most prudent play.

Preflop in SB with As5s facing a middle position raise to 300 from FiveTwo (aggro player based on history) and a cutoff call from Pro (more passive caller). Pot 750, to-call 250 (total bet 300 including my SB 50), getting over 3:1 direct odds with strong implied odds due to suited Ace, wheel/fd potential, and playability. OOP but stacks are deep enough (57BB effective vs Pro/FiveTwo), and Claude BB may or may not defend wide. Folding too tight for A5ss which blocks AA/AKs and has good equity multiway. Raising risks multiway pot OOP or getting 4bet by aggro FiveTwo without huge fold equity. Calling to see flop cheap, control pot, realize equity.

Holding 3h5c in the BB facing a raise to 300 with 2 callers. Getting 5:1 pot odds (200 to win 1000), but 35o is a very weak hand - poor playability multiway, hard to make a strong hand, and when I hit I'll often be dominated. Even with the decent price, this is too weak to continue profitably.

Flop: Ts4c6c with As5s. I have the nut spade flush draw (10 spade outs left) plus an open-ended straight draw (3s/7s for 3-4-5-6 or 4-5-6-7, 8 outs, some overlap with flush outs). Approximately 15-17 clean outs (~50%+ equity vs. most ranges, even multiway ~40%). Pot 1000 OOP first to act in 3-way pot vs. aggro FiveTwo (pre-flop raiser, history of frequent aggression/c-bets/bluffs like Hand 4,7,11,30) and passive Pro (flat caller). Coordinated board with club flush draw possible, hits straight draws. Deep effective stacks (~55BB). Checking risks giving free card to clubs/straights or check-jam overbet; induces raises from draws/value that I can check-raise. But betting semi-bluffs takes initiative, builds pot with equity, charges draws (esp. clubs), denies equity, and leverages fold equity vs. FiveTwo's bluff-heavy style. Vs. calling stations like Pro, realize equity anyway. Half-pot bet balances value (if hit pair/set somehow) and bluff. Sizing 600 (~60% pot) good standard semi-bluff size to get called by worse while setting up turn jam if raised/folded to.

We flopped the nut flush draw (A♣Q♣ on T♠4♣6♣) plus two overcards, and Elon has donk-lead small into a multiway pot. This is a high-equity, high-leverage spot to apply pressure: many of Elon’s lead hands are one-pair/weak made hands or medium draws that hate facing a raise, and raising also denies Pro a cheap peel with overcards/backdoors. If Elon continues or even jams, our equity versus typical continuing ranges (Tx/6x/44/66 plus club draws) is strong enough to proceed, and we set up clean turn shoves on clubs/A/Q and good barreling opportunities on scare cards.

I hold KcQs on a Ts4c6c flop. The action has been explosive: Elon (SB) led out for 600, and FiveTwo (MP) raised to 2100. My hand is merely King-high with a backdoor flush draw. I am facing significant aggression from two opponents. FiveTwo's large raise over a donk bet indicates a very strong hand (sets, overpairs, or nut flush draws) that has me crushed. I have no immediate equity and the price to call is a significant portion of my stack. This is a trivial fold.

Monster combo draw: nut spade flush draw (9 outs) + double gutshot (3x, 2x, 7x for straights 2-3-4-5-6 or 3-4-5-6-7, ~4 additional outs with minimal overlap on 3s) for ~12-13 outs (~50% equity vs. top of range like sets/overpairs, higher vs. draws/Tx). Heads-up OOP vs. FiveTwo's aggressive MP open + flop raise range, which includes many bluffs, weaker Tx, club flush draws, incomplete straights per his history (frequent c-bets/raises on scary boards in hands 4,6,7,11,19,22,24,27,30,37,38,44,46,49,52 where he pressures light or with draws). Pro folded weak. Pot 3700, facing 1500 into 5200 (~3.5:1 direct, great implied). Calling preserves stacks but gives free equity realization to his clubs/draws OOP and allows further aggression. All-in raise (to ~5525 total bet) applies max pressure with huge fold equity vs. his bluff-heavy style, denies equity, builds massive pot (~14k+ if called) where we are live. 49BB effective vs. his 119BB, standard jam spot with this draw strength vs. aggro opponent. Folds better worse draws/T weak, calls value crushed or racing.

Elon’s 3-bet to 5525 effectively commits him (stack shown as 0), so we’re facing an all-in for 3425 more. With pot 8625 and 3425 to call, we need only ~28% equity. On T♠4♣6♣ holding A♣Q♣, we have the nut flush draw (9 outs ≈ 35% by the river) plus additional (sometimes clean) overcard outs versus Tx/6x hands, and we also block villain’s best flushes. Even versus a strong range weighted to sets/two-pair, our flush equity alone is enough to justify a call at these odds, and against any one-pair + draw/semi-bluff components we’re doing very well. Calling maximizes EV and we’re happy to realize our equity in a large pot.

Q3 offsuit is far too weak to open or even limp from UTG 6-handed. It’s dominated by the calling/3-betting ranges behind, plays poorly postflop, and we have no need to risk chips with ~85bb effective. Best EV is to discard and wait for better spots.

I am in Middle Position with 7c 9d, a very weak offsuit hand (trash). The UTG player folded. Given the weak nature of the hand, it is not profitable to play from this position as it is easily dominated and plays poorly post-flop. I will fold.

I am in the Cutoff with King-Deuce offsuit. While I have a decent stack, this hand is too weak to open from this position, especially with a very short-stacked player in the Big Blind (Minni) who is likely to jam over any raise. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop, unopened pot after three folds (UTG, MP, CO). On the button with 12k stack, facing deep SB (Claude 25k) and very short BB (Minni 1.1k). 3s9s is a suited connector with playability, decent for stealing blinds or playing postflop in position. Raising builds pot, applies pressure on blinds, especially short-stacked Minni who may shove wide but we have fold equity and position. Standard raise size 3x BB to 300. Folding too tight on button; limping bad for position/multiway potential.

I have A7s in the SB vs a button open from Elon. This is a solid hand to defend - suited ace with decent kicker. I could 3-bet but calling is fine here since A7s plays well postflop with its suited nature. Minni in BB only has 1100 chips so might squeeze, but that's unlikely with such a short stack. I'll call and see a flop.

I’m in the big blind with J♠9♥ and 11BB facing a button open to 300 with the small blind calling. Folding surrenders a lot of dead money but is too tight; min-raising or shoving is undesirable because the SB’s call removes most of my fold equity and both opponents are deep enough to call, so I’d be committing with a marginal hand. Calling costs me 200 more (total on the street = 300) for a pot that will be ~900 after my call, giving ~4.5:1 effective pot odds to see a flop. With my short stack but a playable, connected hand, the best line is to call, see a flop, and proceed cautiously — fold to heavy postflop aggression without improvement, look to shove or get aggressive only when I hit top pair/strong draws or get a favorable action.

I flopped top pair with A7s on Ah-2d-4h. Great flop for my hand. Board has a heart flush draw so I need to bet for value and protection. Minni only has 900 behind, so any significant bet commits them. I'll lead out around half pot to extract value from worse aces, draws, and any pairs.

Facing Claude's 450 into a 1350 pot on A♥ 2♦ 4♥ with J♠9♥. I have no pair and essentially only backdoor running hearts (~4.2% to hit two hearts) and ~24% to make at least a pair by the river — much of that equity will still be behind an Ace or other made hands. Calling 450 into this pot requires roughly >25% equity, so a call is unjustified. Shoving (900) is too risky — Claude and the preflop raiser will call with many hands that dominate me, so my fold equity is limited. With a short stack and a marginal hand, the correct play is to conserve chips and fold.

Preflop raise from button with suited connector 3s9s was standard for position, fold equity, and playability against blinds. Flop Ah2d4h hits top of range for Claude (SB defender OOP) heavily: any Ax, sets, flush draws (two hearts), wheel potential. My hand has zero showdown value, no draws (spades off, no straight draw as 3s misses 5x for wheel or broadway), pure air. Pot 1350, facing 450 bet (33% pot), calling commits 450 into ~1800 with no equity, multiway history shows Claude can have monsters or draws. Raising as bluff possible in position but stack sizes (my 11.75k, his 25k) and board texture too coordinated for villain to fold Ax/draws. Best play is to fold, preserve stack for better spots in tournament with 63/150 hands played, goal to maximize stack long-term.

I am holding King-Three offsuit (K3o) in the Under the Gun (UTG) position. This is a very weak hand, far below the standard opening range for UTG in a 6-handed game. Opening here would put me out of position against up to 5 opponents with a hand that is easily dominated. Additionally, the Small Blind (Minni) is short-stacked (8.5 BB) and may shove over an open, forcing me to fold immediately. The correct play is to fold.

I am in middle position with King-Seven offsuit. In a 6-max tournament setting, especially with a short stack in the blinds (Minni has only 8.5 BBs), opening with K7o is generally too loose. There are several active and capable players behind me, including Claude with a massive stack and Elon in the cutoff. Given the strength of the remaining players and the marginal nature of my hand, the most prudent play is to fold and wait for a better spot to commit chips.

Preflop in cutoff with 7c4c, a weak suited gapped hand with poor playability and low equity multiway. Folds from UTG and MP mean the pot is unopened (blinds posted, pot 150), but calling 100 risks multiway pot with a speculative hand unlikely to realize equity. Raising with this hand is not standard due to domination risks and reverse implied odds if hit. Short-stacked SB (8.5BB) may shove over a raise, complicating postflop. Deep stacks overall; better to wait for stronger holdings to maximize stack in tournament. Folding preserves chips for better spots.

On the button with T4o, everyone folded to me. This is a standard button steal opportunity. T4o is weak but with position and fold equity against the blinds, a small raise is profitable. Minni is very short-stacked (850) which could lead to a shove, but I still have decent equity vs many hands. Standard 2.5x open.

I’m in the small blind with 9♠4♥ and a short stack (~850 ≈ 8.5 BB). Claude (button) opened to 250 (pot 400) and it’s 200 to call. This is a very weak, unsuited, disconnected hand out of position. Calling to see a flop would leave me crippled (~650 left ≈ 6.5 BB) and likely dominated by the raiser and/or the big blind — playing multi-street poker from this stack and spot is poor. A min-raise is a bluff with almost no fold equity and little fold protection; shoving all-in risks being called by hands with far better equity (BB and button both have enough chips to call profitably). Preserving my stack for a better shove/calling spot or a playable hand is higher expected value here. Fold.

Claude is opening the button to 2.5x with what should be a fairly wide range. From the big blind we’re getting a very good price (150 to win 400 already in the pot), and A6o has enough raw equity and playability (top-pair potential, some wheel/backdoor possibilities) to defend. A 3-bet would inflate the pot out of position versus the chip leader with a dominated-ace candidate, so calling keeps ranges wider and avoids high-variance spots.

On J♥-3♣-9♥ our A♦6♠ has essentially no made value and no meaningful draw (no flush draw, no gutshot, only very thin backdoors). Claude has the range advantage as the preflop raiser on this texture, so leading would just turn our hand into a low-equity bluff that gets called/raised too often. Best is to check and decide based on sizing and his tendencies, keeping the pot small and maximizing our chance to realize whatever equity we have.

Flop is Jh 3c 9h. I have T4o - complete air with just a gutshot if I catch a Q (QJT98). This is a good c-bet spot: I have the betting lead, position, and this board hits my button raising range better than BB's calling range. A small c-bet of ~35% pot should get folds from hands like low pocket pairs and floats while keeping my risk minimal with no made hand.

Claude’s 200 c-bet into 750 is very small and will be made with a wide range, but A♦6♠ on J♥-3♣-9♥ is close to the bottom of our range: we have only one overcard (Ace), no heart, no gutshot, and essentially no meaningful backdoor equity. Calling here means we’ll frequently face turn barrels and be forced to fold anyway, realizing equity poorly out of position. We can still meet MDF with many better continues (pairs, heart draws, gutshots, overcard+backdoor combos), so letting this one go is the lowest-variance, highest-EV choice.

I am UTG with Qs7d. This is a weak hand to open from early position, especially with several aggressive and deep stacks behind me, and a short stack (Minni) who might be looking to jam. Standard strategy dictates folding this hand to preserve chips for better opportunities.

98o is a marginal hand at best from middle position in a 6-handed tournament. Preflop, it plays poorly multiway and has reverse implied odds against better hands. With short-stacked Minni (850 chips) on the button likely to shove wide, and aggressive players like FiveTwo in the blinds who often raise, risking 100 chips (less than 1BB) doesn't offer good implied odds or fold equity if raising. Pot odds are decent at ~1.5:1 but not worth entering a bloated pot OOP with this hand. Deep effective stack (117BB), fold to wait for premium spots to maximize long-term EV over 150 hands.

J2 suited in the cutoff with two folds in front. This is a marginal hand, but from late position with only the short-stacked Minni (850 chips) and the blinds left to act, I can open this. My large stack (26625) gives me flexibility, and suited cards have some playability. Standard 2.5x open to maintain consistency.

I have 850 (≈8.5BB) on the button facing a CO open to 250 (pot 400). With a short stack (<10BB) this is a shove-or-fold spot — calling or a small raise leaves me with awkward post‑flop decisions and little fold equity. 8h6h is acceptable to shove: suited connector with decent equity versus a calling range and it can fold out a lot of opening hands. Shoving maximizes fold equity to steal the pot and avoids tough post‑flop play. Action: shove all‑in (raise to 850).

We’re in the SB facing a CO open (250) and a BTN 3-bet to 850. KJh (suited) has strong playability and enough raw equity versus what is likely a fairly wide BTN 3-bet range (Minni has shown aggression) plus Claude’s wide CO opening range. The price is good: calling 800 more to win 1250 now (and often more when Claude/BB continue) means we don’t need much immediate equity, and the hand can realize well via top-pair+draw/straight/flush potential. A 4-bet from the SB would build a large pot out of position with a hand that’s often dominated when called/5-bet, so I prefer not to bloat the pot. If the BB squeezes or Claude comes back over the top, we can comfortably fold and lose only the call amount. Postflop, default is to check to the preflop aggressor and proceed based on board texture (continue more on high-card/heart/draw-friendly boards, be cautious on K-high/J-high boards when facing heavy pressure).

I am holding 5s 4c in the Big Blind. Facing a raise from the Cutoff, a 3-bet to 850 from the Button, and a cold call from the Small Blind. My hand is extremely weak (unsuited low connector) and performs poorly against the strong ranges implied by this action (3-bet and cold call). Although the pot odds are somewhat tempting due to the dead money, the risk of domination, the fact that the original raiser is still left to act (and could 4-bet), and being out of position in a multi-way 3-bet pot make this a clear fold.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni (850) and a cold-call from FiveTwo. J2s is a weak hand that plays poorly in multiway 3-bet pots. Even with position, I'm unlikely to realize enough equity to justify calling 600 more. My hand has poor playability and I'd be out of position relative to potential strength. Easy fold.

UTG with J5o, a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity against ranges. Standard play is to fold preflop to avoid difficult postflop situations and preserve stack for better spots. Opponent tendencies show aggression from Claude and FiveTwo, but no need to enter pot with trash from early position. Pot is small, but risking chips here is -EV long-term in a tournament setting aiming to maximize stack over 150 hands.
T4o is a very weak hand from middle position. With 4 players still to act behind me, including some aggressive players, there's no value in playing this hand. Clear fold.

Holding A♦K♣ in cutoff with ~20.5 BB — this is a premium hand in position. Limping (calling 100) is too passive and gives the button/blinds cheap odds; I want to take the initiative and get fold equity. Raise to a standard 3x BB (300) — big enough to isolate the blinds and build value, small enough to keep postflop playability with a 20BB stack. If I face a big 3-bet or shove I’ll re-evaluate based on size and player tendencies, but open-raising here is the best long-term +EV line.

Minni is very short (~17.5bb) opening from the cutoff. With 77 on the button, I want to isolate and avoid letting the blinds squeeze or see a cheap flop multiway. A 3-bet also sets up an easy decision versus a likely shove from Minni (we cover and have solid equity versus his get-in range). Sizing to ~3x the open keeps pressure on the blinds and effectively commits Minni.

I am in the Small Blind with Pocket Sixes (6d 6h). The action involves a raise from the Cutoff (Minni, short stack) and a 3-bet from the Button (FiveTwo) to 900. Cold calling a 3-bet out of position with a low pair is generally a losing play, especially given the stack depths (approx 65BB effective with the 3-bettor) which do not offer sufficient implied odds for set mining (typically requiring 15-20x the call amount). Additionally, the original raiser is short-stacked and likely to shove, which would complicate the spot further. Folding is the disciplined and correct decision here.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Deuce offsuit. Minni opened for 300 from the Cutoff, and FiveTwo 3-bet to 900 from the Button. While I have an Ace, it is a very weak one (A2o), and I would be playing the rest of the hand out of position against a 3-bettor. Additionally, Minni's response to the 3-bet is still pending, and she is short-stacked, which could lead to further raises. Given the strength indicated by a 3-bet and my poor positional disadvantage, folding is the most prudent play to preserve my 53.5 BB stack.

I have A♦K♣ and ~17.5BB (1,750). I opened to 300 and the button 3-bet to 900, leaving me to call 600 into a pot of 1,350. With this stack depth and being out of position, I don't want to play multiple post‑flop streets; AKo is too strong to fold and is best used to maximize fold equity here. Shoving my stack (or a full 4‑bet) is the cleaner +EV line: it pressures the button's likely wide 3‑betting range, denies them the cheap odds to call with dominated hands, and simplifies the decision. A min 4‑bet to 1,500 leaves them better pot odds to continue — I prefer to shove and either pick up the pot now or go in flipping/cutting equity against calling ranges.

Minni’s 4-bet to 1750 leaves him essentially committed (looks like ~300 behind), so there’s no real fold equity by jamming—he’ll be priced in to continue. With 77 we have plenty of equity versus a short-stack 4-bet/get-in range, and we’re getting great immediate odds: calling 850 to win a pot of 2800 requires only ~23% equity, which 77 comfortably has even against a fairly tight range. Calling also keeps his range widest and avoids bloating the pot unnecessarily when he’s already effectively all-in.

I'm effectively committed from preflop (I 4-bet earlier and only have ~300 left). Flop 3♠ J♥ 5♣ gives me two overcards (A♦K♣) but no meaningful draw — a five‑card club flush is impossible with only two cards to come and runner‑runner straights are extremely unlikely. Betting my remaining 300 into a 3,650 pot won't fold many better hands and will be called by most pairs/draws; if I face a bet I can call my short stack for excellent pot odds (300 into ~3,950 requires only ~7.6% equity). I check and see the opponent's action — if he bets I plan to commit my remaining chips because the pot odds justify calling to realize my equity.

Minni’s stack is only 300 into a 3650 pot, so any bet is effectively putting him all-in and he’s getting such a huge price that he’ll continue with his entire 4-bet range (overpairs, AJ+, and also AK/AQ). Since we have 77 on J-5-3, we’re often ahead of the unpaired broadways and want to deny their free equity (A/K/Q/T turns can easily swing the hand). There’s no real benefit to checking here given the tiny remaining stack; just take the high-EV line and force realization now.

I’m effectively pot‑committed: I 4‑bet to 1,750 preflop and have 300 left. Flop is 3♠ J♥ 5♣ and the button bets 300 into a 3,650 pot (pot now 3,950). Calling 300 would commit my last chips (total commitment 1,750+300 = 2,050). Pot odds require ~300/(3,950+300) ≈ 7.1% equity to break even. With A♦K♣ I have two overcards (6 outs to make an A or K) and ~24% to hit by the river. That’s well above the break‑even threshold and folding is too tight given my prior commitment. I call (all‑in) to realize my equity.
T5o from UTG is a clear fold. This is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability from the earliest position. With a healthy stack of 264 BBs, I should be patient and wait for better spots.

I have 3♣5♥ in middle position with a 4,250 stack (~42.5bb). Preflop the pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see a flop. 3‑5 offsuit is a very weak, low‑equity hand with poor playability and it will often be dominated or forced out of pots when players behind (CO, BTN, SB, BB) act. The table has shown frequent opens and 3‑bets, so calling 100 or attempting a raise would lead to difficult spots out of position or unnecessary loss vs stronger ranges. Although the call is relatively cheap, my realized/implied odds are poor — folding preserves my stack and lets me look for a better spot to apply pressure or play postflop. Fold.

62s is near the bottom of a CO opening range and performs poorly versus competent blinds because we get dominated often and face a lot of pressure (3-bets/iso) from the Button and especially the deep, active Big Blind. With ~54bb and no dead money beyond blinds, this isn’t a spot where we need to force thin steals; preserving stack and waiting for higher-EV opens is best.

I am on the Button with a weak off-suit hand (T7o). The action has folded to me. While the Button is a prime stealing position, T7o is well below the standard opening range, even for a wide steal. The Big Blind (Elon) has a large stack (116 BB) and has shown capability to play aggressively post-flop (e.g., Hand #43), making a steal attempt with trash risky. There is no need to bleed chips here with 69 BB. Folding is the disciplined and correct play.

Action has folded to me in the Small Blind. I have 97o, which is a weak hand to play out of position. Given that Elon is in the Big Blind with a significant stack and has shown a willingness to be aggressive in previous hands (like the 3-bet in Hand #56), I will choose to fold rather than enter a marginal pot out of position.

UTG with 5♥2♠ — a very weak, unplayable hand. With five players to act and only 42.5 BB, neither an open-raise nor a limp is +EV: raising to 200 invites 3-bets or callers with much stronger ranges, and calling 100 gives pot odds that require ~40% equity (100/(150+100)) which 5-2 offsuit does not have versus typical opening/defending ranges. Limping into a multiway pot with terrible playability is negative EV. Preserve the stack for better spots — fold.

QTo is a marginal-but-playable open in 6-max from middle position, especially with 50bb+ stacks where we can leverage fold equity and play with initiative. Calling is too passive and invites squeezes; folding is a bit too tight given we’re not facing a raise yet. I’ll open to a controlled 2.5x to pressure the blinds and keep the pot manageable, with a plan to fold to most 3-bets from the stronger ranges behind (CO/BTN/SB/BB).

I hold Ten-Eight offsuit in the Cutoff facing a standard 2.5x open raise from a player in Middle Position. T8o is a weak hand that is dominated by the opener's range (which likely includes AT+, pairs, and suited broadways). Although I have position on the raiser, calling invites squeezes from the Button or Blinds, and the hand plays poorly post-flop. There is no strategic merit in calling or bluff-raising this specific holding against this opponent.
I have AKo on the button, which is a premium hand. FiveTwo opened from middle position. I will 3-bet to 800 for value and to isolate, taking advantage of my position.

T2 suited is a weak speculative hand with minimal showdown value and poor post-flop playability, especially out of position in the small blind. Facing a 3-bet to 800 from Flash on the button over FiveTwo's UTG/MP open to 250, Flash's range is strong (likely JJ+, AK, some bluffs but still ahead). Calling 750 into 1200 pot OOP offers poor equity (<30% vs typical 3-bet range) and low implied odds due to hand's capping potential. Squeezing risks multiway pot OOP with dominated range. FiveTwo and BB can still act. Clear fold to preserve stack for better spots.
T4o is a weak hand facing a 3-bet from Flash. Even though I'm getting decent pot odds (700 to win 1200), I'm out of position with a trash hand that plays poorly postflop. Easy fold.

We opened QTo from MP as a steal/initiative play, but facing a BTN 3-bet our hand becomes a dominated, low-realization bluff-catcher out of position. Calling creates a ~2 SPR spot where we’ll frequently be forced into tough decisions with second-best top pairs (dominated by AQ/KQ/QJ and better tens), and we won’t realize our raw equity well. A 4-bet bluff would need an unrealistically high fold frequency given the sizing and effective stacks. Sticking to the preflop plan (open, then fold to most 3-bets) is best here to preserve stack for higher-EV spots.

UTG 6-handed with 7c4d offsuit is far too weak to enter the pot. This hand is dominated when we make a pair, has poor playability, and we’re likely to face frequent isolation/3-bets from aggressive players behind. Preserving 51 BB and waiting for better opens/steal spots is higher EV than limping or opening this combo.

I hold Ace-King suited (Ad Kd) in middle position, which is a premium starting hand. After the UTG player folded, my goal is to build the pot and seize the initiative. Limping would be suboptimal as it invites multi-way pots where high cards lose value. A standard opening raise is appropriate here. Given the stack depths (I have roughly 69 big blinds), a raise to 2.5x the big blind (250 chips) is a standard and effective sizing to isolate opponents and extract value while keeping the pot manageable relative to stacks.

I am holding 9s 2d, which is an extremely weak hand. Pro has raised from middle position to 250. There is no strategic reason to call or raise with this hand in this position. I will fold to conserve my chips for better opportunities.

7s6s is a speculative suited connector with good playability. On the button, I have excellent position against a middle position raise from Pro (stack 6650). The raise to 250 is standard sizing, pot is 400, calling 250 offers about 2.6:1 direct odds but strong implied odds for straights, flushes, or two-pair given deep effective stacks (Pro ~66BB, me 117BB). Folding is too tight preflop in position; 3-betting is suboptimal as 76s performs better multiway or seeing flops. SB (Claude deep 262BB) and short BB (Minni 41BB) still to act, but calling keeps pot small and realizes equity postflop where position shines. History shows Pro calls stations sometimes but mixes aggression; this spots equity realization over showdown value.

8s4h is a very weak offsuit hand. Even with decent pot odds (200 to win 650), I'm out of position against two opponents with a hand that has poor playability post-flop. No suit, no real connectivity. Easy fold.

I have A♥J♥ in the big blind facing a middle‑position open to 250 and a button limp/call. This is a very playable, high-equity hand that does well isolating heads‑up and also has good postflop playability (nut heart backdoor/flush + Broadway). With a ~41BB stack I want to seize the initiative, build a pot when I’m likely ahead of the caller’s range, and apply pressure to both opponents. Raising to 900 (total) gives meaningful fold equity without committing my stack, keeps opponents’ decisions difficult, and leaves ~3250 behind if called (pot ≈2750 if both call → SPR ≈1.2), which is a good SPR to extract value with top pair or a strong draw. A min‑raise or a flat call would invite multiway action and put me in tougher OOP spots; a larger 3‑bet would be fine but 900 balances fold equity and postflop flexibility. If I face a large 4‑bet shove I’ll re-evaluate (likely fold to an unequivocal shove), but overall 3‑bet now to isolate and take the lead.

I hold AdKd (Ace-King suited), a premium hand, in middle position. Facing a large squeeze 3-bet to 900 from Minni (BB, 33.5 BB stack) after my open and a cold call from Elon (BTN), I have a strong value hand that plays well for stacks. Minni's large 3-bet size (9bb) commits a significant portion of their stack. Flatting is an option, but it invites Elon along and risks missing the flop in a low SPR spot. A 4-bet is the most robust line to isolate Minni, deny equity to Elon, and realize my hand's full equity by seeing all five cards. Given Minni's stack size, any standard 4-bet commits them. Shoving (or raising to cover Minni effectively) maximizes fold equity against Elon's capped range and puts Minni to a decision for their tournament life. I block AA and KK. I will move all-in to isolate and maximize pressure.

Preflop, after calling the initial 250 raise from Pro in position with suited connector 7s6s, now facing Pro's 4-bet jam to 6650 total (additional 6400 to call) over Minni's BB 3-bet to 900. Pot is 7850, offering ~2.2:1 direct odds (31% equity needed to break even). Pro's range as initial MP raiser jamming over 3bet is very strong: QQ+, AKo, maybe some AKs, AQs. 76s has poor equity (~15-20%) against this range, no domination issues but low pair/flush/straight potential crushed by sets/top pair. Minni has 3350 behind and may call/shove, but even heads-up vs Pro is a fold. Position irrelevant postflop with all-in. Tournament stack 115BB deep, no need to speculate; fold preserves chips for superior equity realizations later. History shows Pro can be aggressive but this sizing screams premium.

I 3‑bet to 900 with A♥J♥ to isolate and seize initiative, but Pro’s very large 4‑bet to 6650 forces me to commit my entire stack (I must call 3350). The pot is ~7850; calling 3350 would make the final pot ~11,200, so I need roughly 30% equity to break even. Pro’s sizing polarizes heavily toward value (AA/KK/QQ/AK mixes) where AJs is frequently dominated or a big underdog, so my equity is likely at-or-below the break‑even point. Preserving ~33BB and waiting for a cleaner spot is the correct long‑term play here — fold.

I am in UTG position with 7c 9h, which is an unsuited one-gapper. This is a very weak starting hand, especially from early position at a 6-handed table. With 81 big blinds, I have plenty of chips and no need to force action with sub-optimal holdings out of position. The standard and correct play here is to fold.

I'm holding 73 offsuit in Middle Position. This is one of the weakest hands in poker and doesn't warrant an open or a call. Folding is the standard and correct play here to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

KJo suited (Kc Jc) is a strong hand from the cutoff position in an unopened pot. With good position, suited connectors have excellent playability post-flop for straights, flushes, and top pair potential. Standard tournament open-raise size is 3x the big blind (300 total). Opponents behind include deep-stacked button (Claude), but Minni (33BB) and FiveTwo (50BB) are shorter; raising builds pot with equity advantage and fold equity. Limping or calling is too passive; folding is incorrect.

96o is a weak hand that plays poorly even with position. Facing a standard open from Elon in the cutoff, this hand doesn't have good playability - no suited draws, no high card strength, poor connectivity. With a healthy stack lead, no need to get involved with marginal holdings. Easy fold.

I’m in the small blind with K♦4♦ facing a cutoff open to 300. K4 suited is weak and often dominated by better Kx hands; I’ll be out of position postflop and the BB can still act (creating multiway/squeeze risk). Although calling 250 is cheap relative to my 3,300 stack, the hand has poor playability vs a CO open. Preserve chips and wait for a better spot — fold.

Elon is opening from the cutoff to 3x, and we’re out of position with Q3o—an offsuit, disconnected hand with poor playability and heavy reverse implied odds (dominated by almost all Qx he opens). While the pot odds (200 to win 450) are tempting, realizing equity OOP versus a competent/active opener is difficult, and we’ll often be forced into check-fold lines on most flops. A 3-bet bluff with Q3o is also too low quality. Best decision is to conserve chips and fold.

I am UTG with Ten-Nine offsuit. While it's a decent connector, it is generally too weak to open from early position in a 6-handed game, especially with several aggressive and deep-stacked players behind me. Opening here would likely put me in a difficult spot out of position post-flop or force me to fold to a 3-bet. I will wait for a better hand or a better position.

Qd7c is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity, and no strong post-flop potential. In middle position with multiple players behind including aggressive opponents like Claude (deep stack in cutoff) and Minni (button), folding is standard to preserve stack and avoid difficult spots. Tournament stage favors tight play to maximize long-term EV.

T6 suited in the cutoff with two folds ahead. Marginal hand but playable in late position. With a healthy stack of 26k and only 3 players behind (button and blinds), I'll make a standard steal raise. If I get 3-bet, I can fold easily.

5♥3♣ is a very weak, unplayable hand. Claude opened to 250 from the cutoff (standard sized raise) and both blinds still act — likely creating multiway pots or facing a defend/3-bet. Calling 250 or attempting a light 3-bet with 5‑3o here has very poor equity and poor implied odds; with a 3,300 stack (~33 BB) I should preserve chips for better spots and higher EV opportunities on the button. Fold and wait for a hand with real playability.

Claude is opening from the cutoff and covers us by a lot. J3o is too dominated and plays terribly out of position; calling invites reverse implied odds and also leaves Pro in the BB a good spot to squeeze. This hand isn’t strong enough to defend or 3-bet bluff with our stack depth, so we conserve chips and wait for a better spot.

I am in the Big Blind with 8h2h facing a raise to 250 from Claude in the Cutoff. The pot is 400 and I need to call 150. This offers me pot odds of roughly 27%. While 82s has raw equity of about 31% against a standard Cutoff opening range, my ability to realize that equity out of position against the chip leader is low (likely around 60-70% realization). 82s is a bottom-tier hand with poor playability, and despite the attractive price, the reverse implied odds and positional disadvantage make this a fold.

UTG with 7h6h suited connectors. This hand has playability post-flop but is too weak to open or limp from early position in a 6-handed game. Multiway pots likely with 5 players behind, including deep-stacked Claude and short-stacked Minni who might shove. Standard fold to preserve stack and wait for stronger hands or better positions to speculate. Pot odds to call 100 into 150 are decent but doesn't justify entering out of position with a hand vulnerable to domination and raises.
9d5c is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity. In middle position with 4 players behind me, this is a clear fold. No playable equity here.

Cutoff with A♣2♥ and action folded to me. With 33bb I want to seize the initiative and pick up the blinds or play a single-raised pot with position. A2o is playable as an opener here — it has ace-high potential and decent postflop playability. I’ll raise to 3x the BB (300) rather than min-raising to 200 to gain extra fold equity and discourage light defenses from the button/blinds. Plan: if everyone folds I take the pot; if called I’ll c-bet on favorable, dry flops and proceed cautiously multiway. If I face a large 3-bet (≈900+), I’ll usually fold A2o out of position to avoid flipping or dominated situations.

On the button with AJs (AdJd) versus a cutoff open, this is strong enough to 3-bet for value and to isolate the shorter stack (Minni ~30bb). Flat-calling invites squeezes from the SB/BB and gives up initiative; 3-betting also pressures a wide opening range and denies equity to hands that would realize well in position. I’ll use a standard in-position sizing versus a 3bb open (~3x) that still leaves Minni room to make mistakes postflop while being prepared to continue versus a shove due to favorable pot odds and good blocker effects (A/J).

I am in the Small Blind holding 6s 3h, which is an extremely weak hand. The action ahead of me involves a raise to 300 from the Cutoff and a 3-bet to 900 from the Button. Facing significant aggression from late position with a trash hand while out of position is a trivial fold. I cannot profitably call or raise here.

I am in the Big Blind holding Kd 2d. The action started with a raise to 300 from Minni (CO) followed by a 3-bet to 900 from FiveTwo (BTN). While King-high suited has some playability, cold-calling a 3-bet out of position against two opponents is generally a losing play, especially with a weak kicker like the Deuce. My pot odds are approximately 2.7:1, but the likelihood of being dominated or missing the flop entirely is high. FiveTwo has shown significant aggression in previous hands, suggesting their 3-bet range is likely strong or designed to isolate. I will fold and wait for a better spot to commit chips.

I opened to 300 but FiveTwo has 3-bet to 900. Pot is 1350 and it costs me 600 to call (900 total). I have A♣2♥ with 3,000 chips (30BB) and I’ll be out of position to the button post‑flop. Calling requires ~24% equity to break even, but A2o is frequently dominated by a button 3‑bet range (many A‑x, broadways, pairs) and has poor OOP playability. 4‑betting to 1500 or shoving is too committal against a likely strong range. Consistent with my pre‑flop plan to fold A2o to large 3‑bets, I’ll release this hand and preserve my stack for better position/spot.

Q9 suited in diamonds from UTG. This is a borderline hand for UTG - suited broadway connector has some playability with flush potential. With my large chip lead (26k vs next best 11.5k), I can afford to play some speculative hands. Standard open to 2.5x.

Claude opened UTG to 250. I have J♦6♥ in middle position — a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and high chance of being dominated vs an UTG opening range. There are several players left to act (cutoff, button, blinds) who often call or 3‑bet, creating a likely multiway pot where this hand has very little equity out of position. I have ~30bb, so calling 250 or risking a 3‑bet bluff isn’t profitable here. Preserve the stack and fold to wait for a better spot.

AJs is strong enough in the CO versus an UTG 2.5x open to play aggressively. Flatting keeps dominated-A situations in and invites squeezes from the Button/SB/BB; 3-betting denies that, wins the pot often, and gives us initiative/position when called. Claude has shown he can open/fold and also fight back with 4-bets, so we size a clean in-position 3-bet that pressures his wide opens but still allows us to comfortably fold to a big 4-bet. Going to 800 (about 3.2x) is a solid sizing at 50/100 with these stacks.

I am facing a UTG open to 250 and a 3-bet from the Cutoff to 800. My hand is A9o. Cold calling a 3-bet, especially one directed at an UTG opener, with A9o is extremely -EV. My hand is dominated by almost all value hands in their ranges (AK, AQ, AJ, 99+). Even against bluffs, A9o plays poorly in 3-bet pots. The correct play is to fold.

I am holding Ten-Four offsuit in the Small Blind. Claude opened from UTG and FiveTwo 3-bet from the Cutoff. This hand is extremely weak and does not play well post-flop, especially out of position against two players who have shown significant strength. Folding is the only viable option.

Preflop in BB with 7d3d, a speculative suited hand but poorly connected and low. Facing UTG open from Claude (strong range) and a 3-bet to 800 from FiveTwo in CO (also strong polarized range). Pot odds are 1200:700 (~1.7:1), insufficient for this marginal hand OOP. Postflop playability is poor with reverse implied odds against likely better hands. FiveTwo's stack is 4625, so committed somewhat, but no fold equity or value. Standard fold to preserve stack.

FiveTwo 3-bet me to 800 from the cutoff. Q9s doesn't play well OOP against a 3-bet range. With awkward SPR if I call (pot ~1750, FiveTwo has ~3825 behind = SPR ~2.2), I'd be in a tough spot postflop. I have a huge chip lead and don't need to defend marginal hands here. Folding and preserving my stack is the disciplined play.

UTG in a 6-handed game with 8h7s offsuit is too marginal to open. I’m out of position against deeper stacks (Flash, Claude, Elon) and the standard UTG opening range should be much stronger; calling 100 would likely lead to a multiway, OOP flop with a weak hand and poor equity. With a 3,000 (30bb) stack I’ll conserve chips for better spots and avoid marginally speculative hands early — fold.

Minni has folded, so we can attack the blinds with a standard open. Tc9c is a strong enough suited connector in 6-max to raise from middle: it has good playability, can make strong disguised hands, and benefits from fold equity preflop. With ~58bb, I prefer a smaller-but-standard 2.5x sizing to keep the pot manageable versus potential 3-bets while still pressuring the blinds.

I am holding 8d 3s in the Cutoff, which is a very weak unconnected hand (8-high offsuit). An opponent (FiveTwo) has raised to 250 from Middle Position. Given the poor quality of my hand and the aggression from an earlier position, I have no equity to justify a call or a bluff raise. The standard and correct play is to fold immediately to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with QdTh. FiveTwo has raised to 250 from middle position. My stack is around 55 big blinds, which is healthy. Q-10 offsuit is a decent hand to play in position against a single raiser, though it can be dominated. FiveTwo has been fairly active. I could call to play a pot in position, or fold if I think their range is too strong. Given the odds (250 to call into a pot that will be 650 if the blinds fold), calling is a standard play here to see a flop with a hand that has good post-flop playability and position.

Q2o is a marginal offsuit hand with poor playability out of position in the small blind. Facing a middle position raise from FiveTwo (aggressive player) and a button call from Flash, the range is strong enough that Q2o lacks sufficient equity (dominated by Qx, pairs, suited connectors). Pot odds are ~3.25:1 (200 to call into 650), but implied odds are low OOP with this hand. Folding preserves our deep stack (11500) for better spots in a tournament with 74/150 hands played.

K2 suited in the BB facing a min-raise and a call. Getting 4.3:1 pot odds (150 to win 650). K2s has enough playability with suited king - can flop flush draws or top pair. Closing the action makes this an easy call.

Completely whiffed this flop with K2h on 5d 3h 3d. No pair, no flush draw (hearts vs diamonds), no straight draw. I'm first to act with two opponents who showed preflop interest. Standard check here - I have no reason to bet with air and will fold to aggression.

We’re 3‑way on a low paired board (5-3-3). While this texture is generally good for the preflop raiser, multiway dynamics mean opponents continue much tighter and check-raise more value-heavy. With Tc9c we have no pair and only thin backdoors, so a flop c-bet is likely to get called by any 5x/pocket pair/3x and we don’t have great equity when called. Checking back keeps the pot manageable, realizes our equity, and preserves our ability to take stabs on favorable turns if it checks through again.

The flop is 5d-3h-3d, a very dry paired board. Both the Big Blind (Claude) and the pre-flop raiser (FiveTwo) have checked. While I could attempt to take the pot with a bet, checking behind is a prudent play here. It allows me to realize my equity for free and see a turn card. If I hit a Queen or a Ten, I'll likely have the best hand, and if the turn is a blank, I can re-evaluate based on their actions. Given FiveTwo's previous aggression, I want to avoid a check-raise on such a disconnected board.

Turn brings Td putting three diamonds on board. I have K2h with zero diamonds - no pair, no flush draw, nothing. Both opponents checked flop showing weakness, but the diamond turn means anyone with a single diamond has a draw. Bluffing here into two opponents with no equity is risky. Check and see a free river.

We checked back flop in a 3-way pot on 5-3-3, and now the Td improves us to top pair. With two opponents and a now 3-diamond board, we want to (1) get value from 5x and pocket pairs (66–99) that will often call once, and (2) deny equity to overcards and A-high/overcard hands that picked up a diamond or other backdoor equity. At the same time, we shouldn’t bloat the pot too much because trips (any 3x) is possible and will usually continue aggressively. A solid, medium sizing accomplishes value/protection while keeping our range balanced after the flop check-through.

On the turn, the Ten of Diamonds gives me top pair with a Queen kicker. The board is paired (3-3-5-10) with three diamonds. FiveTwo, the pre-flop raiser, checked the flop and is now betting 500 into a pot of 800. My top pair is strong, but given the flush possibilities and the paired board, a raise might only be called by better hands (like trips, a flush, or a higher kicker). Calling allows me to keep the pot size controlled while in position and see what the river brings. If the river is a blank, I can comfortably call another reasonable bet or check back for showdown value.

K2h with no diamonds on a 5d 3h 3d Td board. I have zero equity - no pair, no flush draw, no straight draw. FiveTwo bet 500 and Flash called, showing real interest. Pot odds are 3.6:1 needing ~22% equity but I'm basically drawing to 3 kings against two opponents who likely have pairs or diamond draws. Clear fold.

We improved to a strong two-pair (Tens and Nines) after getting called on the turn, so we should go for clear river value rather than risk a check-back from Tx/5x/pocket pairs. Flash’s turn call weights him toward one-pair hands that can pay off again (Tx, 5x, 66–99) plus some missed diamond draws. A ~1/2 pot sizing targets those one-pair hands while still keeping our bet/fold option intact versus a polar raise that’s heavily weighted to boats/quads (33/55/99/TT or slow-played 3x).

The river card is the Nine of Hearts, which doesn't change much on the board of 5d-3h-3d-Td-9h. I have top pair with a Queen kicker. FiveTwo, who was the pre-flop raiser, checked the flop and has bet both the turn and the river. His river bet of 900 into a pot of 1800 gives me 3-to-1 odds to call. Given my position and the fact that most overpairs (AA-JJ) would likely have bet the flop, his range consists of some better Tens (AT), some worse Tens (KT, JT), or potential bluffs from missed diamond draws. With a strong top pair and favorable pot odds, calling is the most profitable decision here.

6-handed UTG I want to open fairly tight, especially with several opponents who have shown willingness to 3-bet (and one very big stack in the blinds who can apply pressure). Q4 suited is too dominated (by any Qx continuing range) and doesn’t realize equity well from out of position; limping is also a leak. I’ll pass and preserve stack for better spots.

I am in Middle Position with Jh 6h, which is a weak suited connector (two-gapper). With 50/100 blinds and no antes, this hand is below the standard opening range for this position, especially with active players and big stacks behind me. The risk of being dominated or 3-bet is too high to justify an open. Folding is the correct conservative play to preserve chips for better spots.

Ace-Nine offsuit is a standard opening hand from the Cutoff in a 6-max game, especially after the action has folded to me. With a stack of 39 big blinds, a 3x open raise to 300 allows me to exert pressure on the button and blinds while remaining within a normal opening range for this position.

A3s is a strong speculative hand on the button against a cutoff open from Flash (3x raise, standard size). Excellent position, suited ace with good playability, straight/flush potential, and ace blocker for bluffs. Pot odds are favorable (calling 300 into 450, ~1.5:1 direct, better with dead money). Button defends wide vs CO opens in tournaments. Prefer calling to realize equity in position rather than 3-betting a non-premium hand, as it keeps pot smaller OOP stacks (Minni short at 2900 may shove, Claude deep). Flash has been active but not overly aggressive recently; heads-up in position postflop is ideal. Folding is too tight. Pot will be ~750 heads-up if blinds fold.

Pocket 8s in the SB facing a raise and a call. This is a classic set-mining spot. With 750 in the pot and 250 to call, I'm getting 3:1 odds. 88 plays well multiway trying to flop a set. A 3-bet would likely only get called by better hands. Calling to see a flop is the most profitable play here.

Call 300 total (put 200 more). I have A♦T♥ in the big blind facing a CO open to 300 with the button and SB already calling — a multiway pot. The pot is ~1000 so calling 200 gives excellent pot odds (~5:1) and ATo has reasonable showdown equity and can hit top pair or useful draws. I’m relatively short (2900) so I don’t want to bloat the pot or risk a shove/3-bet by Flash (3600) by raising here out of position. Calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me play postflop for value or fold to big aggression if I miss. Plan: continue aggressively with top pair/top kicker or strong draws, take a cautious line otherwise.

I have 88 on a 9h5h6d board - second pair with a gutshot straight draw (need a 7). The board has a heart flush draw. In a 4-way pot, my hand is decent but vulnerable. Betting for value/protection could work, but with 3 opponents I'm likely to face resistance from better hands or draws. Checking allows me to pot control and see how the action develops. If someone bets small I can call; if it gets heavy I can fold without losing more.

I have A♦T♥ on 9♥-5♥-6♦ in a multiway pot (Flash opened, Button and SB called). I currently have no pair and only backdoor possibilities — runner-runner heart flush (~4.2%) and two-card straight/paired-outs. Chance to pair an A or T by the river is ~24% combined, but on the flop I have very little showdown/value equity and I’m out of position with a short stack (2,700) into a ~1,200 pot. C-betting here is thin and likely to be called by pairs/draws; a raise would put me in a tough spot. I’ll check to control the pot, see a turn for improvement, and reserve aggression for when I pair (or face a very small bet). I will call a modest bet with the redraw potential but fold to large pressure.

I have top pair, top kicker (TPTK) on a wet board featuring both straight and flush draws. After the blinds checked to me, checking behind would allow opponents to realize equity for free, which is dangerous given the connectivity of 9-5-6. I will bet for value and protection. A sizing of 700 (nearly 60% of the pot) is appropriate to charge drawing hands and extract value from middle pairs or weaker nines.

A3o is a complete air hand on this flop. No pair, no straight draw (gutshot to 4 is too weak and unlikely), no flush draw. Board is highly coordinated with heart flush draw and multiple straight draws (78, 47 for wheel/OESD, 34). Flash's CO raise preflop followed by a 37% pot bet as first to act postflop after checks shows strength or semi-bluff, but our ace high has minimal showdown value multiway against calling ranges from Claude/Minni or Flash's value range. Pot odds are poor given low equity (~20-30% vs polarized range at best), and position doesn't help enough with such weak holdings. Preserve stack for better spots; folding avoids difficult postflop decisions OOP stacks ahead.

I have second pair (88) on 9h5h6d with a gutshot. Flash bet 700 into 1200. The board is wet with flush and straight draws. Minni still to act behind me with 2700 chips. Getting about 2.7:1 but my hand is marginal - I beat bluffs/draws but lose to overpairs and top pair. With someone behind and a coordinated board, continuing here is likely -EV. Fold and preserve stack.

I have A♦T♥ on 9♥-5♥-6♦, out of position and relatively short (2,700). Flash (CO) c-bet a moderate 700 into the pot — he can have a wide c-bet range in this multiway pot but also lots of pairs and heart draws that currently beat me. I have ~24% to pair an A or T by the river plus a small runner-runner heart chance. Raising OOP with no pair would be a thin bluff and would commit a large portion of my stack; folding surrenders reasonable equity. Calling keeps the pot controllable, lets me realize equity and preserves stack flexibility. Plan: call to see the turn; if I pair the A or pick up a strong draw I can be aggressive, but I’ll fold to large turn shove/barrel if I don’t improve.

Heads-up vs Flash with the pot at 2,600 and my stack only 2,000. I hold A♦T♥ on 9♥–5♥–6♦–3♦. I have only 6 clean outs (3 Aces, 3 Tens) — ~13% to pair on the river — and there is no longer a feasible heart flush. Checking to the river gives me a low chance to improve and lets Flash take the initiative; with my short stack I need spots that use fold equity. If I shove all-in for 2,000, Flash must call 2,000 to win a pot of 6,600, so he needs ≈30% equity to profitably call. I don’t have 30% raw card equity, but I will beat a portion of his missed c-bets/overcards and will pick up the pot when he folds marginal pairs/draws. Given the heads-up dynamic, effective stacks and the need to accumulate chips, an all‑in shove maximizes my chances to pick up the pot now or double up when called. I will push all-in for 2,000.

I hold Top Pair, Top Kicker with Ace-Nine on a board of 9-5-6-3. My opponent, Minni, has led out for 2,000 chips on the turn, which is nearly a pot-sized bet and puts her all-in. While this 'donk' bet could signal a very strong hand like a straight (7-8) or two-pair (5-6, 9-5, 9-6), the pot odds and the relative strength of my hand make this a mandatory call. The pot is 4,600 and I need to call 2,000, meaning I need roughly 30% equity to break even. Given that Minni can also be playing semi-bluffs like heart draws or straight draws (7-8, 4-7) or even weaker nines, folding TPTK in this situation would be overly tight and exploitable. I will call to see the river.

I am UTG with KJo in a 5-handed game. This is a standard opening hand from this position. Raising to 300 (3bb) is consistent with my previous opening sizes throughout the tournament and allows me to take the initiative. Limping is generally suboptimal as it invites more players into the pot and gives the blinds a better price to see a flop. I will open and evaluate based on my opponents' reactions.

A9h is a strong suited ace hand with excellent playability, blocker value to AA/AK/AQ, and good equity against Flash's UTG opening range, which may be somewhat wide based on observed aggression in prior hands (e.g., Hand 50). Position is UTG+1, slightly OOP to the raiser but with 112 BB effective stack depth allowing post-flop maneuvering. Calling 300 into 450 offers solid implied odds (~2.15:1 direct, better with multiway potential from loose tendencies like frequent check/calls seen across history). Folding wastes equity; 3-betting risks spew OOP vs potential 4-bets from deeper stacks like Claude. Prefer calling to realize equity, control pot OOP if check-fold flops, or exploit post-flop with draws/straights/flushes.

Ts8s is a playable suited connector on the button with position. Two players in for 300 each creates good pot odds and implied odds. With my big stack and position, calling to see a flop is profitable - I can realize equity well and potentially flop a strong draw or made hand.

We’re in the small blind facing a 3x open plus two callers, creating a multiway pot where we’ll be out of position to everyone postflop (and the BB can still wake up). K2s has decent raw equity, but it realizes very poorly here: when we make a king we’re often dominated, and our suited value (flushes) is too infrequent to compensate without position. A squeeze is also unattractive—likely to get called by at least one player, forcing us into a bloated pot OOP with a weak, dominated king. Despite the tempting pot odds to complete, folding preserves stack and avoids a low-realization spot.

I am in the Big Blind facing a raise from UTG (Flash) and two callers (Elon and Claude). The pot is currently 1050, and I need to call 200 chips to see the flop, giving me pot odds of approximately 5.25:1. While the price is attractive, my hand (T8 offsuit) is very weak. I will be playing out of position against three opponents, one of whom showed strength by raising from early position. T8o has poor playability, suffers from reverse implied odds (domination by better Tens or Eights), and will struggle to realize its equity. Despite the odds, the likelihood of winning this multi-way pot is low, and preserving chips is preferable to making a marginal loose call.

I have flopped the absolute nuts with Quad Jacks (Four of a Kind) and a King kicker. The board is Jd Jc Js and I hold the Jh. Since I was the pre-flop aggressor, checking here looks like a 'give-up' on a very coordinated/strange board, which might induce a bluff from Elon or Claude. Alternatively, if they have a pocket pair or an Ace, they now have a full house and will likely bet for value or check back to see a turn. My goal is to maximize value from a hand that is currently unbeatable. Betting now might fold out all their air. I will check to trap.

The flop of Jd Jc Js is a dream board where every player holds at least trip jacks using the community cards. My Ah9h provides excellent kickers (A-9), making it the best unpaired kicker hand possible among trip jack combinations, beating any non-ace kicker hand (max K-Q) outright. I lose only to the narrow quads range (J♥x, unlikely from Flash's UTG raise) or full houses (QQ+, KK, AA, which are possible but Flash's check suggests caution rather than trapping). Better ace-kicker trips (AT+, AQ, AK excluding AJ/J♥) are in Flash/Claude's ranges but less dense in UTG/Button calling range, and Flash's check reduces likelihood of monsters. No flush/straight draws possible (rainbow board, poor straight connectivity). With pot 1050 and checked to me OOP to Claude but IP on Flash, a 2/3 pot bet of 700 extracts value from worse kicker hands like KQ/KJ/A8 etc., which comprise much of ranges post-flop. Builds pot with ~60-70% equity vs calling ranges, protects against unlikely villainous draws, and denies free cards. Check risks giving free cards or multipway pot OOP; betting maximizes EV in deep tournament spot (109BB eff). Size leaves fold equity vs bluffs while inviting calls from weaker trips.

Board is JJJ - everyone has trip jacks. Only pocket pairs (full house) or JJ (quads) beat me. My T kicker is mediocre but calling is standard here. Raising only gets called by better hands. Elon's bet could be value with a pocket pair or just betting with any kicker. Calling to see the turn is correct.

I have flopped the absolute nuts with Quad Jacks. After checking to the pre-flop callers, Elon has bet 700 and Claude has called. This indicates that at least one of them likely has a hand they want to protect or value bet, such as a pocket pair (making a full house) or an Ace. If I raise now, I strongly telegraph that I have the last Jack, which might cause them to fold everything but the highest pocket pairs. By calling, I keep the pot multi-way, hide the strength of my hand, and allow them to continue betting or bluffing on the turn. I want to keep their ranges as wide as possible to maximize value.

I have the absolute nuts with Quad Jacks. On the flop, Elon showed aggression by betting 700 and Claude called. My check-call on the flop successfully disguised my hand strength while keeping the pot multi-way. The 5h on the turn is a complete brick that shouldn't change the dynamic of the hand. If I lead out now, I risk folding out their bluffs or medium-strength hands like pocket pairs or Ace-high which are now full houses. By checking again, I am giving Elon the chance to barrel again or Claude the chance to take a stab at the pot. My goal is to maximize the pot for a significant river value bet or raise.

Trip jacks with Ah9h kickers remain very strong, likely best unpaired kicker hand (only beaten by AJ+, AK, AQ with A kicker better but AJ has J kicker tie then 9 vs their second). 5h turn adds flush draw to my hand (two hearts), providing additional equity (~35% flush draw + trips equity), semi-bluff protection, and nut flush blocker (Ah). No straight draws complete meaningfully (e.g., 4-6 gutshot unlikely strong pre). Flash's check-check pattern suggests weaker trips/medium strength rather than nuts (Jh x rare UTG, AA/KK/QQ would likely bet flop for value/protection). Claude's call OOP to me indicates decent but not monster kickers (e.g., QJ/KJ/TJ/Ax low). Pot 3150 deep effective vs Flash (~5700 behind post-700 call), my stack allows aggression. Continue thin value betting 2/3 pot (2100) to extract from worse kicker trips/draws, build pot OOP, deny equity to any unlikely villain draws. Checking risks free river card to villainous flush/completions or check-jam setups; betting maximizes EV with 70%+ equity vs calling ranges.

Board is JJJ5 - everyone has trip jacks. Elon's large 2100 bet (2/3 pot) suggests strength - likely a pocket pair for a full house or a strong kicker. My T kicker is mediocre, losing to any pocket pair and high cards like A/K/Q. However, pot odds are 5250:2100 (~2.5:1), needing ~28% equity. Folding trip jacks feels too weak, but raising only gets called by better hands. Flash still to act behind. Calling to see the river and control pot size is the play.

I hold the absolute nuts with Quad Jacks. My previous check-call on the flop successfully kept both Elon and Claude in the hand. On the turn, Elon has increased his bet size significantly to 2100 and Claude has called again. There is now over 7,000 in the pot and I have 6,500 remaining. If I raise now, I am heavily representing the last Jack, which might force Elon or Claude to fold hands like mid-pocket pairs or Ace-high (which are currently full houses). By calling, I maintain the disguise of my hand, keep the pot multi-way, and allow Elon to potentially fire a third bullet on the river or Claude to over-commit. My plan is to get my remaining chips in on the river.

I hold the absolute nuts with Quad Jacks. Throughout the hand, I have played a deceptive line by check-calling, allowing Elon to lead the betting. Elon bet 700 on the flop and 2100 on the turn, and Claude has been sticky, calling both times. The pot is now 9450, and I have 4400 remaining in my stack. Since Elon has been the aggressor, checking the river is the best play to induce him to either shove his remaining chips or fire a third bluff. If I lead out, I might fold out Claude's medium-strength hands or Elon's bluffs. By checking, I maximize the chance of one of them committing their remaining chips to the pot. I will check-raise all-in against any bet.

River Tc is a brick that doesn't complete straights or flushes meaningfully (hearts draw missed, no new flush possible, 6-9/7-Q gutshots unlikely in ranges). Board trips J's make FH only possible with pocket 55s (unlikely pre from Flash UTG raise/Claude call OOP) or TT (possible but rare, Flash check-river reduces trapping likelihood). Quads extremely narrow (Jh x). My Ah9h trips J's have elite kickers: top Ace primary, strong 9 secondary, crushing Kx/Jx/Qx/broadway (vast majority of ranges that called flop/turn), losing only to AA/AK/AQ/AT (~few % of UTG/Button ranges) or monsters (<2%). Flash's consistent check-calls (flop/turn/river check) profile weaker trips/draws rather than nuts (trappers bet streets for value/protection). Claude's OOP calls cap at medium strength (AJ+/worse Ax). Pot 9450, Flash 4400 (short), Claude deep 22k, my 8100. Value bet 4200 (~45% pot, covers Flash stack) extracts max from worse kicker trips (KQ/KJ/A8 etc. call), fold equity vs bluffs/Air, no thinner than prior streets. Checking risks cheap showdown/free hero call or missing value; jamming Flash while pricing Claude call maximizes EV in deep MTT spot (hand 76/150, build stack aggressively with premium).

River brings the Tc - now I have Jacks full of Tens (JJJ TT) with my Ts! This dramatically changes the hand. I beat all trip jacks and all pocket pairs below TT. I only lose to AA/KK/QQ (full houses) or quads. Elon's continued aggression could indicate a pocket pair for a full house, but he would've likely 3-bet AA/KK/QQ preflop. His flat-call preflop and subsequent betting suggests a medium pocket pair or strong kicker. Raising only gets called by hands that beat me. With a full house, calling is correct for pot control and to catch bluffs/worse value bets.

I hold the absolute nuts with Quad Jacks. My strategy of check-calling on every street has worked perfectly, as Elon has fired three barrels and Claude has called along. With Elon betting 4200 and Claude calling on the river, the pot is massive. I have 4400 total in my stack. Raising all-in to 4400 is the only move here to extract the final 200 chips from my opponents. Since Claude has already called 4200, he will certainly call the additional 200 into a pot of this size, and Elon is likely to call as well if he has any piece of the board or was value betting a full house.

River Tc completes any Tx hand to full house JJJTT, beating my trips JJJ with kickers A-10c-9h-5h. Flash's check-call flop/turn then check-raise all-in over my river value bet (after Claude call) is very strong, likely Tx (e.g., ATo+, KTs, JTs in UTG range), TT/55 pockets, or AA/KK/QQ full houses that slowplayed. Quads Jh rare. However, range not 100% monsters: includes weaker trips like KQ/KJ/Qx/Ax low-medium kickers that check-call streets but raise river for thin value/protection or semi-bluff (unlikely pure air given history). My A9o blockers to AA/AK/AQ; equity ~25-40% (0% vs FH, ~50% vs trips Ax/Kx polarized range). Pot 22250 to call 200 (~0.9% equity breakeven) + Claude's 18275 behind (likely calls extra 200 with strong hand, adding dead money; may even induce fold) makes call massively +EV. Stack 3900 (39BB), hand 76/150; folding loses massive overlay while calling risks only 5% stack for potential double-up or more. Prior value line committed; call extracts max EV.

I have Jacks full of Tens (JJJ TT). Flash min-raised to 4400 and Elon called. I only need to call 200 into a pot of 22,450 - that's 112:1 pot odds. Only AA/KK/QQ beat me with higher full houses. This is a trivial call.

6h8c is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability and low equity multiway. UTG position requires a strong range for opens or limps; folding preserves stack for better spots. Opponents have deep stacks, and history shows aggressive play post-flop, so no value in entering with marginal hand. Standard fold to maximize long-term EV.
2s6d is a junk hand - no high cards, not suited, poor connectivity. Easy fold from early position.

5-handed on the button after two folds, JTo is comfortably in my open-raising range. Raising rather than limping leverages position, picks up the blinds often, and keeps initiative against two wide defending ranges. I’ll use a moderate 2.5x sizing to apply pressure without bloating the pot too much versus the deeper BB; if faced with a larger 3-bet (especially from Flash), I’m generally folding this offsuit, non-premium holding.

I am in the Small Blind with K7o facing a Button raise to 2.5BB from FiveTwo. Although I am getting reasonable pot odds to call (calling 200 to win a pot of 400), playing King-Seven offsuit out of position against a button opener is generally -EV. The hand is easily dominated by better Kings and plays poorly post-flop. Additionally, the Big Blind is yet to act and could squeeze, forcing me to fold anyway. Given the stack depth (75+ BBs), position is crucial, and defending here is likely to lose chips in the long run. Folding is the standard play.

I am holding 4s 3h in the Big Blind. FiveTwo has raised to 250 from the Button. While I am getting decent pot odds to call (150 to win a total pot of 550), 43o is one of the weakest possible starting hands and plays very poorly out of position. There is no reason to defend with such a low-equity hand, especially when I have a significant chip lead and want to maintain it. Folding is the clear professional play here.
Q3o is a very weak hand, especially from UTG position where I need a tight opening range. Not worth opening or limping with this hand.

Ts8h is a marginal offsuit holding that plays poorly from UTG+1 in a 5-handed lineup: it’s frequently dominated by the hands that continue versus an open (Tx, 8x, broadways), and it doesn’t realize equity well out of position postflop. Limping invites isolation from the button and especially the aggressive big stack in the SB, while opening risks getting 3-bet and forced to fold or play a bloated pot with weak equity. With no dead money beyond blinds and no need to defend, folding is the highest-EV/lowest-variance choice here.

I am holding 2s 4d on the Button, which is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Although I am in position and action has folded to me, the hand has very poor playability and equity. The Small Blind has a large stack and may defend or 3-bet, and the Big Blind is short-stacked and could shove. Attempting to steal with 7-high offsuit is not a profitable play in this spot. I will fold.
